name: H3 (Uber)
pricing_notes: Fully open source (Apache 2.0); no API costs

performance.notes: Sub-microsecond per cell operation; C library with Python/JS/Go/Java/Rust bindings

agent_readiness.notes: Uber's hexagonal hierarchical spatial indexing system. Maps globe into hexagonal grid at multiple resolutions (0-15). Convert lat/lng to H3 cell, find neighbors, compute distances, compact/uncompact regions. Multiple language bindings (Python, JS/TS, Go, Java, Rust). Industry standard for geospatial aggregation and partitioning in data systems.

gotchas: Hexagonal grid has non-obvious edge cases (pentagons at icosahedron vertices)
H3 indexes not human-readable — requires tooling for visualization
Grid distortion increases at extreme latitudes (near poles)
best_when: Spatial data aggregation (heat maps, density analysis) at scale
Location-based feature engineering for ML models
Geospatial partitioning of large datasets in data warehouses
Proximity search and geofencing with efficient neighbor lookups
avoid_when: Exact polygon boundary matching (H3 approximates polygons)
Simple point-in-polygon tests (PostGIS or Shapely simpler)
Navigation and routing (not designed for routing)
summary: H3 is Uber's hexagonal hierarchical spatial indexing system. Maps the globe into hexagonal cells at 16 resolution levels. Python/JS/Go/Java/Rust bindings. Industry standard for geospatial data aggregation, ML feature engineering, and dataset partitioning. Sub-microsecond operations. Best for large-scale spatial analysis, proximity search, and location-based features. Not designed for routing.
